full_description:
Global Business Development Associate | Cybersecurity Services
We are looking for a highly motivated Global Business Development Associate to help expand the client base of Cyberspatz, a cybersecurity consulting firm focused on helping businesses identify security gaps, reduce risk exposure, and strengthen their security posture.

This is a remote opportunity for professionals experienced in B2B lead generation, sales outreach, appointment setting, and client acquisition across international markets.

What You'll Do
Identify and engage decision-makers including CEOs, Founders, CTOs, CIOs, CISOs, and IT Managers.
Generate qualified leads across North America, Europe, Australia, the Middle East, and APAC.
Conduct outreach via LinkedIn, email, cold calling, and other channels.
Schedule discovery meetings with qualified prospects.
Build and maintain prospect pipelines.
Collaborate with our team to convert opportunities into long-term clients.
Ideal Candidate
Proven experience in B2B sales, business development, or lead generation.
Experience selling technology, SaaS, IT services, or cybersecurity solutions.
Strong communication and relationship-building skills.
Familiarity with CRM tools, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Apollo, Clay, Instantly, or similar platforms.
Ability to work independently and deliver measurable results.
Existing network of business decision-makers is a major advantage.
Services We Offer
Vulnerability Assessments
Penetration Testing
Web & Mobile Application Security Testing
API Security Assessments
Security Audits
Cloud Security Reviews
Security Awareness & Advisory Services
Compensation
Attractive commission structure on every closed deal.
Performance-based bonuses for consistent results.
Long-term collaboration opportunity for top performers.
To Apply

Please include:

Relevant business development or sales experience.
Industries and regions you have worked with.
Lead generation methods and tools you use.
Expected commission structure or compensation model.
Examples of previous results achieved.

We are seeking professionals capable of generating real business opportunities, not just lead lists. If you have a track record of opening doors and closing deals in the B2B technology sector, we'd like to hear from you.

full_description:
I’m running a Teensy 4.1 that delivers a 0 – 3.3 V square wave at 80 kHz. For my signal-transmission stage I must boost that logic-level waveform to a clean +6 V amplitude, powered from an existing 13 V rail. Because the downstream chain mixes analog and digital circuitry, frequency stability, waveform accuracy, and strong noise immunity are all critical; edges need to stay sharp without ringing or overshoot, duty cycle must remain intact, and added jitter should be negligible.

Here’s what I’d like from you:

• A proven conversion topology (discrete driver, high-speed op-amp, gate driver, or any suitable approach) that takes 0 – 3.3 V in and produces a solid +6 V square wave at 80 kHz. 
• Schematic with full component values, part numbers, and a concise explanation of how each stage maintains edge integrity. 
• SPICE or other simulation results showing amplitude, rise/fall times, and noise 

I already have the 13 V supply available on the board, so the design should draw from that rail and avoid additional isolated supplies. As long as the final output is a steady +6 V square wave at 80 kHz with minimal distortion, I’m open to clever component choices or integrated solutions.

This project is only for qualified veterinary doctors or certified dog trainers. I manage [login to view URL], a growing reference site for dog owners, and I want every article to reflect current veterinary consensus while still feeling friendly and easy to follow.

full_description:
Project Title: Design a Custom Jukebox Controller PCB (Drop-In Replacement)

Project Overview

I am developing a modern Linux-based digital jukebox platform and require a custom controller PCB to replace an obsolete legacy controller board.

The original board is available and can be supplied to the successful freelancer for measurement and connector identification.

The objective is not to copy the original electronics. The objective is to design a modern replacement board that fits the same physical location, uses the existing wiring harnesses and provides the functionality required by the new software.

Requirements

The replacement PCB must:

* Match the physical dimensions of the existing board.
* Use the same mounting hole locations.
* Position connectors so the existing wiring harness can be reused.
* Be designed using modern, readily available components.
* Include complete design files, schematics, PCB layout and firmware source code.

Core Functions Required

Coin and Credit Functions

* Coin mechanism pulse input.
* Coin mechanism enable/inhibit output.
* Coin mechanism status LED control.
* Credit button input.
* Service button input.

Audio Functions

* Stereo line-level audio input from PC motherboard front-panel audio header.
* Stereo audio output to existing amplifier.
* Software-controlled volume control.
* Audio mute control.
* Amplifier enable/disable control.
* Audio switching and routing capability.

Microphone Functions

* Microphone input.
* Microphone enable/disable.
* Provision for future microphone priority and music ducking.

Remote Control Functions

* Infrared remote receiver input.
* Report remote-control button presses to host software.

Communications

* USB connection to Linux PC.
* USB serial communications interface.
* Commands and events exchanged with host software.
* Firmware update capability over USB.

Additional Inputs and Outputs

* Status LEDs.
* Optional cabinet door switch input.
* Expansion inputs and outputs for future features.

Preferred Hardware

Open to recommendations, however ESP32-S3, Raspberry Pi Pico 2 or similar modern microcontroller platforms are preferred.

Deliverables

* Complete schematic.
* PCB design files (KiCad preferred).
* Bill of Materials (BOM).
* Gerber files.
* Firmware source code.
* Assembly documentation.
* Prototype-ready design suitable for manufacture by JLCPCB, PCBWay or similar.

What Will Be Provided

* Original controller board for measurement and connector identification.
* High-resolution photographs of both sides of the board.
* Connector and wiring information identified during software development.
* Ongoing communication during development and testing.

Please provide examples of previous PCB design, embedded systems, audio electronics, USB device or controller board projects completed.

full_description:
I need a skilled Unity 3D developer to build a full-featured car-racing game. The core experience must feel fast and authentic, so realistic physics is non-negotiable; I expect tight handling, believable collisions, and visible weight transfer. Competitive play is central to the concept, therefore a smooth, real-time multiplayer mode has to run reliably on desktop and mobile. 

Tracks should be based on a real-world map I will supply (road layout, elevation data, and reference photos are ready). You can suggest the most efficient workflow—whether that’s importing GIS data, sculpting terrain directly in Unity, or leveraging external tools like Blender—so long as the finished circuit looks and drives like its real counterpart.

I plan to use standard Unity pipelines: C#, URP/HDRP where appropriate, and either Photon, Mirror, or Unity Netcode for networking (choose the option you are most comfortable optimising). Feel free to integrate any proven racing-specific assets for vehicle physics, as long as the end result can be customised later.

Deliverables 
• Playable prototype with one fully modelled track, at least two car prefabs, and functional online lobbies 
• Clean, well-commented Unity project files and C# scripts 
• Brief setup document explaining scene hierarchy, networking configuration, and how to add new tracks or vehicles 

The project will be reviewed against frame-rate stability, accuracy of the physics, and seamless multiplayer synchronisation. If everything aligns, we will extend the scope to additional cars, deeper customisation, and matchmaking features.

full_description:
I’m looking for a UniFi-savvy network professional to dive into my existing router configuration and sort out two persistent problems: sluggish overall performance and clients that randomly refuse to connect. The access points and switches are fine; the router is the bottleneck.

Here’s what I need you to do: 
• Analyse the current UniFi Network Controller settings (VLANs, DHCP, firewall, traffic shaping, IDS/IPS) and pinpoint any mis-configurations that could be throttling throughput or blocking devices. 
• Run live diagnostics to replicate the slow-speed and “can’t join” scenarios, then apply fixes—whether that involves firmware rollback/upgrade, interface tweaks, or rewriting rules. 
• Optimise WAN/LAN performance so wired and wireless devices see consistent speeds and stay connected. 
• Document each change so I can retrace the steps later and keep the network healthy.

Success is when the network sustains normal throughput under load, every authorised device joins first time, and the UniFi dashboard shows clean, stable statistics for at least 24 hours after your tweaks.

If you’re confident with SSH access, UniFi OS / Controller, and advanced troubleshooting tools like Wireshark or iperf, I’d love to get this resolved quickly.

full_description:
I’m producing a 30-second VFX/CGI introduction that will launch our brand, Uncaged. The piece needs to move fast, hit hard, and leave viewers feeling motivated and intrigued—setting the tone for everything that follows. Our core audience is young adults, so the visuals must feel fresh and contemporary without slipping into kiddish territory. Think “realistic cartoon”: stylised proportions and bold colour, yet grounded lighting, textures and physics so nothing ever looks toy-like.

Storywise, the spot will chart the brand’s journey from confinement to full-on liberation, using kinetic camera moves, particle bursts and dynamic typography to echo that sense of breaking free. There’s no live-action plate; every frame will be built in CG, then composited for a polished finish. If you’re comfortable bouncing between Blender, Maya or Houdini for modelling and simulation, and finessing the final grade in After Effects, you’ll feel right at home.

Deliverables 
• 30-second master file (ProRes 422 HQ, 4K & 1080p) 
• Clean text-free pass for localisation 
• Layered project files and assets, organised and labeled 

Acceptance will be based on: 
• Clear conveyance of motivation and intrigue throughout the narrative arc 
• Consistency with the “realistic cartoon” aesthetic—stylised but believable 
• Smooth pacing, precise sync to the supplied soundtrack, and error-free renders 

If you’re ready to unleash something truly uncaged, let’s talk timelines and milestones so we can get this rolling.
